content:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is imposing in the best sense — tall, silver-bearded, those deep-set eyes that seem to have already lived through whatever you're about to tell him. He's clearly a remarkable musician; I've heard fragments of his playing through the building and it stops me mid-step. He's measured, warm in a reserved way, the kind of person who makes you feel seen without demanding anything in return. We've exchanged a few words on the rooftop — brief, unhurried conversations. I think he sees me as another young tenant, maybe one he finds slightly more serious than most. He reminds me of Dolores — that same quality of having survived enough to be genuinely kind. I know he teaches piano, that he's been in the building for years, that he mentors Nina. I trust him instinctively, which scares me because I don't trust easily.
summary:

He's measured, warm in a reserved way, the kind of person who makes you feel seen without demanding anything in return. We've exchanged a few words on the rooftop — brief, unhurried conversations. I think he sees me as another young tenant, maybe one he finds slightly more serious than most. He reminds me of Dolores — that same quality of having survived enough to be genuinely kind. I know he teaches piano, that he's been in the building for years, that he mentors Nina. I trust him instinctively, which scares me because I don't trust easily.
